project: 二胡识谱 - Erhu Score Trainer
description: Remaining work for 二胡识谱 iOS app: complete the practice loop, enhance audio engine, add content and polish. Phase 1 = MVP (stories 1-5), Phase 2 = enhanced features (6-15), Phase 3 = platform polish (16-17). Core functionality (Phase 1) is the primary goal; exit after Phase 1 if desired.

userStories.title: Xcode project configuration for iOS build
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a proper Xcode project setup so the app can be built and run on iOS 18+ Simulator.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate .xcodeproj from Package.swift, or configure Package.swift so 'xcodebuild -scheme ErhuApp -destination platform=iOS Simulator' succeeds
All 15 Swift source files compile without errors targeting iOS 18+
Info.plist is correctly bundled: display name '二胡识谱', microphone usage description in Chinese
App launches on iPhone Simulator (iOS 18+)
Microphone permission prompt appears on first launch of practice view
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use 'swift package generate-xcodeproj' or create minimal xcodeproj. iOS Simulator runtime must be installed in Xcode.

userStories.title: YIN pitch detection algorithm for erhu
userStories.description: As a user, I want accurate pitch detection optimized for erhu's timbre (portamento, vibrato) so the app correctly recognizes notes I play.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace autocorrelation in Sources/ErhuApp/Audio/AudioEngine.swift with YIN algorithm
YIN threshold parameter defaults to 0.15, configurable
Detect fundamental frequency range C3-C7 (65Hz-2093Hz)
Note detection latency under 100ms for sustained notes
Accurate within ±20 cents for sustained notes
Handle erhu portamento (滑音) gracefully: no false triggers during pitch slides, only register when pitch stabilizes within 50 cents for >80ms
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implement YIN in pure Swift with Accelerate for performance. See de Cheveigné & Kawahara (2002) for algorithm reference.

userStories.title: Real-time score follower
userStories.description: As a user, I want the score display to highlight the current note as I play so I can follow along without losing my place.
acceptanceCriteria: Current note highlights in real time (yellow background) when user plays the correct pitch
Score follower advances one note when user plays within ±50 cents of target for >100ms
Follower pauses advancing when amplitude drops below threshold (>500ms of silence)
Follower automatically skips rest notes (degree==0) without requiring input
Visual indicator shows follower state: green dot = listening, yellow dot = waiting for input, gray dot = idle
When user reaches end of score, show performance summary automatically
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Update PracticeView.swift. The follower runs in a Task with periodic checks. Add state enum for follower status.

userStories.title: Real-time audio visual feedback
userStories.description: As a user, I want immediate visual feedback on pitch accuracy so I can correct my intonation while playing.
acceptanceCriteria: Each note cell shows judgment color: green background = correct (±50¢), red = wrong note, orange = correct note but out of tune (>50¢ off)
Arrow indicators: ↑ red arrow when sharp (>50¢), ↓ blue arrow when flat (<-50¢)
Cents deviation label below note: '+23¢' or '-15¢' in small text
Current frequency displayed in Hz: '440.0 Hz'
All feedback updates in real time (<200ms from note detection)
Smooth color transitions (0.3s animation) to avoid jarring changes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Update ScoreView.swift NoteCell and PracticeView.swift. Reuse PitchJudger.Judgment struct.

userStories.title: Performance summary after practice
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a summary of my practice session after finishing so I can track my progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Summary screen appears automatically when score follower reaches end of score or user taps '结束'
Shows: total notes played, correct count, accuracy percentage, duration
Breakdown by note degree (do/re/mi/fa/sol/la/si) showing accuracy per degree
Accuracy for most recent attempt preserved until next practice
Buttons: '再练一次' (retry same score) and '选择其他曲目' (go to score library)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: New view: PerformanceSummaryView.swift. Take PitchJudger.PerformanceResult as input.

userStories.title: Jianpu text parser
userStories.description: As a user, I want to import scores from plain text jianpu notation so I can practice custom pieces.
acceptanceCriteria: New service JianpuParser parses text like '1 2 3 5 6 5 3 2 1 | 5 6 5 3 2 | 1 -' into Score model
Numbers 1-7 = scale degrees, 0 = rest, spaces=note separator, | = bar line
Dots after number raise octave (1. = high do), dots before lower (1 = low do)
Dash after number = half duration (5- = two beats), underscore = double duration (5_ = four beats)
Produces valid Score object with correctly partitioned measures
Shows parse errors with line/position on invalid input
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: New file: Sources/ErhuApp/Services/JianpuParser.swift. Pure logic, no UI.

userStories.title: Score editor and importer UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create and edit scores directly in the app using jianpu text.
acceptanceCriteria: New '自定义曲谱' section in ScoreLibraryView with '新建' button
Text editor view with jianpu input field and live preview of parsed score
'保存' button saves custom score to ScoreService (in-memory array, survives session)
Custom scores show a badge '自定义' in the library list
Edit existing custom scores by tapping pencil icon, delete by swiping
Empty state: '还没有自定义曲谱，点击新建开始'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: New view: ScoreEditorView.swift. Update ScoreLibraryView to show custom vs built-in scores.

userStories.title: A/B section loop practice
userStories.description: As a user, I want to loop a specific section of a score so I can drill difficult passages.
acceptanceCriteria: Long-press on a note sets loop point A (start), long-press another sets loop point B (end)
Loop region highlighted with orange background on the score
When playback reaches B, it jumps back to A automatically
Loop count stepper: '循环 3 次' (default infinite until user stops)
'取消循环' button clears loop markers
Loop markers persist across practice sessions on the same score
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Update PracticeView.swift and ScoreView.swift. Add loopStartIndex and loopEndIndex state.

userStories.title: Tempo control and metronome
userStories.description: As a user, I want to control practice tempo and hear a metronome beat.
acceptanceCriteria: Tempo slider (40-200 BPM) in practice view, adjustable before and during practice
Metronome toggle button: ON = tick sound at current tempo
Metronome click on every beat (4 clicks per measure in 4/4)
Visual flash (circle pulse) on beat 1 (downbeat)
Beat indicator lights: 4 dots that light up sequentially per measure
Tempo value shown as: '♩ = 80'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Metronome audio can use AVAudioPlayer with short tick sound file or generate via AVFoundation.

userStories.title: Practice session recording
userStories.description: As a user, I want to record my practice session audio so I can review my playing later.
acceptanceCriteria: Record button in practice view: red circle, pulses when recording
Audio recorded to app's Documents directory as AAC/m4a files
Recording metadata (date, score title, duration) saved alongside audio file
Recording list accessible from practice view's toolbar
Recording stops automatically when practice ends, or manually with stop button
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use AVAudioRecorder. Metadata in JSON or Core Data. Store in Documents/Recordings/.

userStories.title: Playback comparison with synchronized score
userStories.description: As a user, I want to play back my recording with the score synchronized so I can see where I made mistakes.
acceptanceCriteria: Recording list shows: date, score title, duration, accuracy
Tap recording to play back: audio plays + score highlights notes in sync with original judgments
Playback speed control: 0.5x, 1x, 1.5x
Seek bar to jump to any point in recording
Swipe to delete recording with confirmation alert
Empty state: '还没有录音记录'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Need to store note-by-note judgment timestamps during original practice for sync.

userStories.title: Core Data persistence for practice records
userStories.description: As a developer, I want practice records persisted so users can track their progress across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Core Data model: PracticeRecord entity (date, scoreTitle, accuracy, duration, totalNotes, correctNotes)
NoteDetail entity (parent PracticeRecord, noteIndex, degree, wasCorrect, centsOff, timestamp)
Save practice results to Core Data automatically when performance summary is shown
Load practice history on app launch for charts and statistics
Migrate ScoreService from in-memory array to Core Data (optional: keep built-in scores in-memory)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create Core Data stack in new file PersistenceController.swift. Use NSPersistentContainer.

userStories.title: Progress charts and statistics
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my accuracy trends and practice habits so I can track improvement over time.
acceptanceCriteria: New '进度' tab or section showing charts
Chart 1: Accuracy per practice session (last 7/14/30 days or all), line chart
Chart 2: Accuracy breakdown by note degree, bar chart
Chart 3: Daily practice time, bar chart
Streak counter: '连续练习 5 天' with flame icon
Stats: total practice time, total sessions, best accuracy, average accuracy
Use Swift Charts framework
Empty state: '还没有练习记录，开始练习吧！'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: New view: ProgressView.swift. Add to TabView in ContentView.swift. Requires Swift Charts (iOS 16+).

userStories.title: Daily practice check-in and reminders
userStories.description: As a user, I want daily practice reminders so I maintain a consistent practice habit.
acceptanceCriteria: Local notification scheduled at user-configurable time (default 19:00): '今天练琴了吗？'
Check-in: first practice of the day shows a congratulatory toast '🎵 今日打卡成功'
Calendar view showing practice history: green dots on days practiced
Notification permission request on first app launch (in onboarding)
Toggle notification on/off in Settings
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use UNUserNotificationCenter. Calendar view with custom SwiftUI or UICalendarView (iOS 16+).

userStories.title: Extended score library with 12+ pieces
userStories.description: As a user, I want more erhu pieces to practice organized by difficulty.
acceptanceCriteria: Add scores: 二泉映月, 良宵, 空山鸟语, 光明行, 江河水, 月夜, 三门峡畅想曲
Difficulty filter buttons: 全部/入门/初级/中级/高级
Filter persists during session (remembers selected filter)
Search by title returns results in <100ms
Favorites: tap heart icon on score row, filter by '收藏'
Scores grouped by difficulty in library list with section headers
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Update ScoreService.swift with new scores. Add difficulty property to Score model. Update ScoreLibraryView with filters.

userStories.title: iPad and landscape adaptive layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want to use the app on iPad and in landscape orientation for a better score-reading experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Use size classes: compact (iPhone portrait) vs regular (iPad and landscape) layouts
Score view shows 2 measures side-by-side in regular width
Practice controls side panel on iPad (controls on right, score on left)
ScoreLibraryView uses grid layout (2 columns) on iPad, list on iPhone
All views properly laid out without overlapping in landscape on iPhone (Keyboard avoid)
Supported orientations in Info.plist: portrait + landscape for iPad, portrait for iPhone
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use @Environment(\.horizontalSizeClass). Adaptive layouts with ViewThatFits or conditional modifiers.

userStories.title: Onboarding tutorial flow
userStories.description: As a new user, I want a guided introduction so I understand how to use the app.
acceptanceCriteria: 3-screen onboarding on first launch: (1) 欢迎 - app purpose, (2) 简谱入门 - how to read jianpu, (3) 如何练习 - practice workflow and feedback reading
Screen 2 shows a mini jianpu example with labels (do re mi fa sol la si)
Screen 3 shows mockup of practice view with feedback callouts
'Skip' button on every screen
'开始使用' button on last screen dismisses onboarding permanently
Onboarding only shows once (UserDefaults flag 'hasSeenOnboarding')
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: New view: OnboardingView.swift. Use TabView with page style. Check flag in ErhuApp.swift.
